pages.full_text:
we shopped a bit & went back to the van for lunch. There was a small park by the river.

        We drove on to the Burrell Collection. Wonderful mediaeval carvings - alabaster from Nottingham. Chinese porcelain. Mediaeval interiors. Tapestries. Tonic enjoyed running in the huge field.

        We drove to the Cathedral which had just shut, & then walked down to the Fire [strikethrough: Station] Station Restaurant. Friendly, good soup, but the garlic bread & pancake were burnt.

        Rain just before bed, & the night & next morning were clear.

        __Tues Aug 3rd__

dodgems  We left at 10.30, going N.W. We stopped at [?dodgems] Inveraray. (We went up the west side of Loch Lomond). Then north to Loch Awe. We stopped to see St. Conan's Kirk, light. Carvings. Zigzagging to Oban.

        We reach Oban, 1¾ hour wait for the ferry. Oban is like Barmouth, but bigger.

        Very near the ferry terminus, on the 'Island of Mull, is our campsite, __Craignure__ *** Odd white plastic tents for wash rooms, "carpeted cottage-style tents", washing machine room, reception & drying tents. Lovely rock roofs for John & Charle[s]
pages.notes: Text continues from a previous page (begins mid-sentence). No handwritten page number visible at top-left. The word 'dodgems' (or 'dodgers') appears written twice in the margin/inline near 'Inveraray' — possibly a note about a fairground/dodgem cars seen at Inveraray. There is a strikethrough on the word 'Station' before 'Restaurant' (i.e. 'Fire Station Restaurant' — the word 'Station' was struck through and rewritten, suggesting a correction). Year anchored to 1999 based on the 'Wales May 1999' section heading visible on the right page and the volume's date range; Aug 3rd 1999 was indeed a Tuesday, which corroborates the year assignment. Text is cut off at bottom with 'John & Charle[s]' — presumably continues on the next spread.

pages.full_text:
__Wales May 1999__

[glued-in photograph: man in wide-brimmed hat sitting in a camping chair holding a dog, camper van in background]

to play in. Eve did a load of wash & it nearly dried overnight.

        Glorious weather. V. hot. Breezy sunshine all day.

        __Wed 4th Aug__
        We relaxed in the morning. The boys played ping pong Early lunch & caught 1.10 train from Craignure to Torosay Castle. We walked
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '15' at top-right corner. The heading 'Wales May 1999' is written in green ink at the very top of the page; this is anomalous as the body text continues the Scotland/Mull narrative from the left page (mentioning Craignure and Torosay Castle on Mull). The heading likely marks the start of a new chapter/section on the following pages rather than describing the content on this page. The body text continues mid-sentence from the previous page/left page ('to play in.' — referring to rock roofs for the children to play on). A photograph is glued in showing a man in a wide-brimmed hat holding a dog by the camper van — no handwritten caption. Text is cut off at the bottom ('We walked') and continues on the next spread.
